ticket_title: Tax Appears as 0 on Accessory Item Details

test_cases.name: TC-1: Accessory details panel shows non-zero tax in a taxed state [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Activation in a US state that charges sales tax; at least one accessory in the cart. | Test Data: state = TX (8.25% combined), accessory = Case $14.99, role = Store Rep, storeType = COR

steps.action: Add the accessory to the cart.
Open the Accessory Item Details panel / drawer.
Read the displayed tax value.
steps.expected: The tax field shows the computed sales tax (≈ $1.24 for $14.99 @ 8.25%) and not $0.

test_cases.name: TC-2: Tax on details panel matches the cart and the receipt for the same accessory [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Cart with at least one taxable accessory and one device. | Test Data: state = CA (~9% combined), accessory = Charger $19.99

steps.action: Note the tax shown in the Accessory Item Details panel.
Open the cart summary and note the per-line tax.
Complete checkout and inspect the receipt's accessory line tax.
steps.expected: All three surfaces show the same tax value down to the cent.

test_cases.name: TC-3: Tax-exempt state correctly shows $0 (regression) [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Activation in a no-sales-tax state (NH, OR, MT, DE, AK). | Test Data: state = OR, accessory = Case $14.99

steps.action: Add the accessory.
Open the Accessory Item Details panel.
steps.expected: The tax field shows $0.00 — but as a legitimate, computed zero (not a missing or stuck value) and the cart and receipt agree.

test_cases.name: TC-4: Multi-accessory cart — each line shows its own correctly-computed tax [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Cart contains 2+ accessories at different prices. | Test Data: state = NY (~8% combined), [Case $14.99, Charger $19.99, Screen Protector $9.99]

steps.action: Add all three accessories.
Open each Accessory Item Details panel in turn.
Compare the tax per line.
steps.expected: Each line's tax matches `price × state rate` independently and no shared / sticky value bleeds across lines.

test_cases.name: TC-5: Promo'd / discounted accessory — tax computed on the discounted price [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: An accessory has a promo or Edge Coupon discount applied. | Test Data: accessory = Case $14.99 with $5 Edge Coupon, state = TX

steps.action: Apply the coupon / promo to the accessory.
Open the Accessory Item Details panel.
steps.expected: Tax is computed against the post-discount price (e.g., $9.99 × 8.25%) and the details panel reflects that — not $0 and not the pre-discount value.

test_cases.name: TC-6: Re-open of the panel keeps the correct tax (state persistence) [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Accessory in cart with computed tax visible. | Test Data: state = TX, accessory = Charger $19.99

steps.action: Open Accessory Item Details and confirm the tax value.
Close the panel.
Re-open the panel.
Force a hard refresh of the cart.
steps.expected: Tax remains correct across panel re-open and cart refresh; no transient $0 flicker is observed.

test_cases.name: TC-7: 911, USF, and regulatory fees are NOT folded into the accessory tax display [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Activation includes plan-level fees (911 tax / USF / regulatory recovery fee). | Test Data: state = TX, accessory = Case, rate plan that triggers 911 + USF

steps.action: Add the accessory.
Open Accessory Item Details.
Compare the displayed accessory tax to the plan-level fees on the receipt.
steps.expected: The accessory tax shown is sales tax only; 911 / USF / regulatory recovery are not double-counted into the accessory line.
